title: FAFSA and financial aid
description: practice understanding of the purpose of FAFSA, identifying components of financial aid award.

assessment_items.question_content:
Many colleges and universities require applicants to fill out a Free Application for Federal Student Aid (FAFSA) before applying.

**What is the purpose of FAFSA?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: it serves as a college application
it determines how much a family should contribute to a college education
it lists potential scholarships for students

assessment_items.question_content:
Many colleges and universities require applicants to fill out a Free Application for Federal Student Aid (FAFSA) before applying.

**Why do colleges ask for a completed FAFSA?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: to decide what classes a student should take
to collect information for college research

assessment_items.question_content:
Many colleges and universities require applicants to fill out a Free Application for Federal Student Aid (FAFSA) before applying.

**What is the reason for colleges needing the FAFSA?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: to assess suitability for clubs or sports

assessment_items.question_content:
A financial aid award letter, is a document that colleges and universities send to admitted students who have filed the [[☃ definition 1]]. This letter details the financial aid package that the school is offering to the student. 

**Which of these are listed in the award letter?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.definition: Free Application for Federal Student Aid

assessment_items.question_content:
A financial aid award letter, is a document that colleges and universities send to admitted students who have filed the [[☃ definition 1]]. This letter details the financial aid package that the school is offering to the student. 

**Which of these are listed in the award letter?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.definition: Free Application for Federal Student Aid

assessment_items.question_content:
After filling out the [[☃ definition 1]], potential college students will receive a financial aid award letter. This letter informs each applicant about the financial aid package that the school is offering them. 

**Which of these are listed in the award letter?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.definition: Free Application for Federal Student Aid

choices.content: all fees to attend the college

assessment_items.question_content:
An important part of the financial award letter is Net Cost.

**What does the Net Cost amount represent?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the cost of attendance minus any grants, scholarships or other aid received

assessment_items.question_content:
An important part of the financial award letter is Net Cost.

**How is the Net Cost determined?**

Net Cost = [[☃ dropdown 1]] - [[☃ dropdown 2]]
hints: The Net Cost of college is the amount the student ultimately needs to pay in order to attend the school.
To calculate Net Cost, we start with the *cost of attendance*. This amount includes tuition, housing, and books, and represents the cost before any financial aid or other adjustments are made.
*Scholarships and grants* are educational money sources that reduce the college attendance cost. Students use this money, which they don't have to repay, to offset the school attendance cost.
Net Cost = $\boxed{\text{cost of attendance}}$ - $\boxed{\text{scholarships and grants}}$.

assessment_items.question_content:
An important part of the financial award letter is Net Cost.

**Which two amounts are used to calculate the Net Cost?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

To complete the [[☃ definition 1]], applicants generally need the provide certain information.

**Which of the following is essential for filling out the FAFSA?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.definition: Free Application for Federal Student Aid

assessment_items.question_content:
To complete the [[☃ definition 1]], applicants generally need the provide certain information.

**Which of the following is essential for filling out the FAFSA?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.definition: Free Application for Federal Student Aid

choices.content: records of untaxed income, if applicable
parents' financial information, if the applicant is a dependent student
letters of recommendation or personal essays

assessment_items.question_content:
To complete the [[☃ definition 1]], applicants generally need the provide certain information.

**Which of the following is essential for filling out the FAFSA?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.definition: Free Application for Federal Student Aid

choices.content: the exact college and major you plan to pursue

assessment_items.question_content:
In order to continue to receive financial aid, you must fill out a Free Application for Federal Student Aid (FAFSA) every year.

**What are other requirements for continued financial aid?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

In order to continue receiving financial aid you must enroll in at least two classes each semester.

**What are other requirements for continued financial aid?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: have A's  in all your classes

assessment_items.question_content:
In order to continue receiving financial aid you must maintain academic honesty.

**What are other requirements for continued financial aid?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
